sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed
before = last_transition.before_frame.segmentation
after = last_transition.after_frame.segmentation

print("=== Key objects before ===")
for n in before['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('b', 'S', 'W') and n['pixels'] <= 70:
        center_r = sum(p[0] for p in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        center_c = sum(p[1] for p in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        print(f"  {n['color']} {n['pixels']}px ~({center_r},{center_c})")

print("\n=== Key objects after ===")
for n in after['nodes']:
    if n['color'] in ('b', 'S', 'W') and n['pixels'] <= 70:
        center_r = sum(p[0] for p in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        center_c = sum(p[1] for p in n['boundary']) // len(n['boundary'])
        print(f"  {n['color']} {n['pixels']}px ~({center_r},{center_c})")

# Check bottom bar
ascii_after = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print(f"\nBottom bar: {ascii_after[63]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Clicking the blue shape just decreased the timer bar (64 → 62), same as clicking on the purple square in level 1. So clicking the blue shape doesn't do anything useful either.
Let me try clicking on one of the sky blue (S) dots. These are the scattered single pixels. Maybe clicking them does something similar to how clicking G dots moved the purple square in level 1.
Let me try clicking on one of the sky blue dots, say the one at (37,18).
